interpretation: The published PM weight of 10 is approximately forty times the protocol-matched 10-percent gradient-scale weight.
This explains why the published-weight arm strongly increased severe forecast area but caused a 52.27-percent validation-MSE increase and large SUCR losses.
The median PM-versus-MSE gradient cosine is positive and larger than the previously observed tail-area-versus-MSE cosine, suggesting that PM supplies a less tail-specific auxiliary direction.
